DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF EDDY FRANCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION AND EMPLOYMENT FORM INTERROGATORY 217.0 AND FOR AN AWARD OF A MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL DENISE TRANI-MORRIS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,872.50 IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 217.0 IS DENIED. VERIFIED AMENDED RESPONSES TO THE RFAS SHALL BE SERVED WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 AND SUBMIT BY EMAIL TO JUDGE PRO TEM PETER CATALANOTTI TO PCATALANOTTI@FMGLAW.COM.

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF EDDY FRANCO'S MOTION TO QUASH AND FOR AN AWARD OF A MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST DEFTS AND THEIR COUNSEL DENISE TRANI-MORRIS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2380.00 IS GRANTED IN PART. BOTH PARTIES CITE WILLIAMS V. SUPERIOR COURT (2017) 3 CAL.5TH 531 FOR THE STANDARD TO BE APPLIED. ACCORDINGLY, THE SUBPOENA ISSUED BY DEFENDANTS TO PRIOR EMPLOYERS, IKON AND KIRKLAND & ELLIS SHALL BE LIMITED TO JOB DESCRIPTION, APPLICATION FOR EMPLOYMENT AND DATES OF EMPLOYMENT AND NON-PRIVATE INFORMATION. PRODUCTION WILL BE MADE TO PLAINTIFF FIRST. PLAINTIFF IS TO PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG. THE SUBPOENA TO PLAINTIFF'S SUBSEQUENT EMPLOYER, CANON, SHALL BE LIMITED TO JOB DESCRIPTION, COMPENSATION, EARNINGS, AND BENEFITS. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEPHANIE KRMPOTIC; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-15-2019 Vacated

Notice Re Mtn To Quash And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defts And Their Counsel Denise Trani-Morris In The Amount Of $2380
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew Stephenson of Kuchan & Stephenson, Telephone (510) 649-1130, appearing for Plaintiff Eddy Cabrera Franco;
Nicole E. Forde of Jackson Lewis P.C., Telpehone (415) 394-9400, appearing for defendants Vendor Direct Solutions et. al.;

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with a modification as follows:

PLAINTIFF EDDY FRANCO'S Motion To Quash And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defts And Their Counsel Denise Trani-Morris In The Amount Of $2380.

Plaintiff's motion is granted in part. Both parties cite Williams v. Superior Court (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531 for the standard to be applied. Accordingly, the subpoena issued by defendants to prior employers, Ikon and Kirkland & Ellis shall be limited to job description, application for employment and dates of employment and non-private information. Production will be made to plaintiff first. Plaintiff is to provide a privilege log. The Subpoena to plaintiff's subsequent employer, Canon, shall be limited to job description, compensation, earnings, and benefits.

Order that is approved to form is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Employment Form Interrogatory 217.0 And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defendants And Their Counsel Denise Trani-Morris In The Amount Of $1,872.50
Off Calendar Jul-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew Stephenson, Kochan & Stephenson, appeaering for plaintiff Eddy Cabrera Franco, Telpheon 51-649-1130;
NIcole E. Forde, Jackson Lewis, appearing for defendants vendor Direct Solutions et al, Telephone 415-394-9400;

Ruling - Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF EDDY FRANCO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Employment Form Interrogatory 217.0 And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defendants And Their Counsel Denise Trani-Morris In The Amount Of $1,872.50 is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. RFA 1: Defendant's objections "assumes facts not in evidence," "lacks any time period or foundational facts," "calls for speculation," vague and ambiguous, "calls for a legal conclusion," and argumentative are overruled. Defendant argues in its Opposition that "Its inquiry never reached this step as VDS never received information sufficient to discern that Plaintiff's family had a 'serious health condition' for purposes of this statute." (Opp., 6:21-22). If Defendant takes the position that its inquiry never "reached this step," defendant has sufficient information to answer the RFA as to whether or not plaintiff was entitled to California Family Rights Leave. 4: Defendant's objections "assumes facts not in evidence," "lacks any time period or foundational facts," "calls for speculation," "presupposes'", vague and ambiguous are overruled. Defendant shall admit or deny this request. 5: Defendant's objections "assumes facts not in evidence," "lacks any time period or foundational facts," "calls for speculation," "presupposes'," and argumentative are overruled. Defendant shall admit or deny this request. 6: Defendant's objections "assumes facts not in evidence," "calls for speculation," "presupposes'", argumentative are overruled. Defendant shall admit or deny this request. 13, 14: Defendant's objection that the request is "vague and ambiguous'" is overruled. Defendant's attempt to change the question is stricken as improper. Defendant provided a denial of these requests. Therefore, no further responses are ordered. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatory 217.0 is DENIED. Verified amended responses to the RFAs shall be served within two weeks of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied.
Prevailing party to prepare an order approved as to form and that complies with CRC 3.1312 and submit by email to Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
